channel.title: Hurricane Melissa on The Guyana Brief
channel.description: Recent content in Hurricane Melissa on The Guyana Brief

item.title: Yard Report — Thursday, April 16, 2026
item.description: Six months after Melissa, Westmoreland still dark. UHWI owes $40 billion in taxes. Carnival was nice though.

item.title: Cousin Leroy Seh — Jamaica, Monday April 6, 2026
item.description: Cousin Leroy, calling from the Bronx, is very proud of January's crime numbers and not worried about the fuel price.

item.title: The Yard Report — Jamaica Brief, Monday April 6, 2026
item.description: Jamaica's weekly news with the bark left on. JACDEN scandal, fuel prices up 20%, and Jamaica still cleaning up after Melissa.

item.title: Caribbean Brief: Jamaica's Hurricane Budget, T&T Radar Gone, Guyana Plays Football & The US Removes Military Gear from Tobago
item.description: Jamaica tables its Hurricane Melissa budget, the US quietly removes military radar from Tobago, Guyana faces Belize in football, and Antigua is hosting the Commonwealth summit in November. Monday Caribbean.

item.title: Caribbean Brief: Jamaica Counting Hurricane Damage, T&T Gets a US List, and Sandals Is Spending Big
item.description: Tuesday's regional roundup: Jamaica's budget fight, Trinidad's US persons-of-interest list, Sandals drops $200M, and Caribbean AIDS deaths fall 60%.
